blocks.text: DTC P0234: Excess turbocharger boost pressure

The turbocharger system has its boost pressure controlled by the PGM-FI ECU. If boost pressure rises abnormally, the PGM-FI ECU cuts fuel and controls throttle opening to protect the engine. If the boost pressure remains above the specified pressure for more than the specified time, the PGM-FI ECU judges it as a failure and stores DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

items.text: The lower engine speed limit for determining abnormal high pressure for engine protection.
When the intake manifold pressure is determined to be abnormally high for engine protection and continues for more than 2 seconds.
West gate control SOL.V. open sticking
Turbo charger Actuator piping failure (clogging, disconnection)
Disconnection of cord (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and intake pressure sensor
Disconnection of cord (PB line) between PGM-FI ECU and intake pressure sensor
PGM-FI ECU internal failure (barometric pressure sensor)
Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.
Engine speed [engine rpm] 3,250 rpm or more and throttle opening 41° or more for at least 2 seconds.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
